title: Collector
content:
{{CharHub|Collector|Taneleer Tivan}} {{Character |image = CollectorGotgTextlessPoster.jpg |real name = Taneleer Tivan |alias = {{Alias |codenames = Collector{{Ref|TTDW}}}} |species = |gender = Male |affiliation = {{Affiliation|TVN}} |movie = ''[[Thor: The Dark World]]'' ([[List of Post-credits Scenes|mid-credits scene]])
''[[Guardians of the Galaxy (film)|Guardians of the Galaxy]]''
''[[Avengers: Infinity War]]'' (illusion) |tv movie = {{Infobox List|{{Collapse|''[[The Guardians of the Galaxy Holiday Special]]'' (mentioned)}}}} |comic = {{Infobox List|''[[Guardians of the Galaxy Prequel Infinite Comic]]''
''[[Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2 Prelude]]''
{{Collapse|''[[Avengers: Infinity War Prelude]]'' (flashbacks)
''[[Avengers: Endgame Prelude]]'' (mentioned)}}}} |book = ''[[Guardians of the Galaxy Read-Along Storybook and CD]]''
''[[Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2: Rocket's Rules]]'' |actor = [[Benicio del Toro]] |status = Alive}} {{Quote|I present to you Taneleer Tivan, the Collector!|[[Carina]]|Guardians of the Galaxy (film)}} '''Taneleer Tivan''' is the head of the [[Tivan Group]] and the keeper of the largest collection of interstellar fauna, relics and species in the galaxy, which led to his moniker as the '''Collector'''. Presiding over [[Knowhere]] and its lucrative [[Exitar]] mining colony, Collector established his [[Collector's Museum|museum]] there. In [[2013]], he was given the [[Reality Stone]] by the [[Asgardians]], which sparked his desire to collect the remaining [[Infinity Stones]]. The [[2014|following year]], he attempted to purchase the [[Power Stone]] from the [[Guardians of the Galaxy]], although these plans fell through. In [[2018]] he was attacked by [[Thanos]], who despoiled Knowhere in order to obtain the Reality Stone. Following the [[Blip]], Tivan sold Knowhere to the Guardians of the Galaxy. ==Biography== ===[[Tivan Group]]=== ====Building the Collection==== {{Quote|It's quite a collection he has."
"He likes to collect.|[[Volstagg]] and [[Carina]]|Guardians of the Galaxy Prequel Infinite Comic}} Taneleer Tivan, along with the rest of his kind, made his appearance in the Universe shortly after its beginning. He has a brother who would go onto be known as the [[Grandmaster]]. Over the course of the centuries, he became the leader of an intergalactic conglomerate known as the [[Tivan Group]]. Due to his complete obsession with collecting different objects and specimens, Tivan was given the title "Collector"; he controls a massive intergalactic [[Collector's Museum|trove]] containing a vast collection of both objects and living specimens.{{Ref|GotG}} ===Collecting Infinity Stones=== ====Meeting the Asgardians==== {{Quote|[[Reality Stone|One]] down, [[Infinity Stones|five]] to go.|Collector to [[Carina]]|Thor: The Dark World}} [[File:CollectorFirstScene-TTDW.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Tivan personally greets [[Sif]] and [[Volstagg]]]] In [[2013]], two [[Asgardians]], [[Sif]] and [[Volstagg]], arrived at Tivan's [[Collector's Museum|museum]], bringing with them the [[Infinity Stones|Infinity Stone]] known as the [[Aether]], which had been recovered by [[Thor]] after his [[Battle of Greenwich|battle]] against [[Malekith]] on Earth. [[Carina]] introduced the Asgardian warriors to her master, which was followed by Tivan greeting them. Sif insisted upon finalizing their deal so they could leave the museum, as she was somewhat appalled at the sight of female workers imprisoned in specimen cages. [[File:Normal thor-darkworld 5351.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Tivan being given the [[Aether]] for safe-keeping]] Tivan agreed, and asked the two why they decided not to keep it secure in the [[Odin's Vault|vault]], to which Volstagg replied that the [[Tesseract]] was already safe on Asgard, noting that it would be unwise to keep two Infinity Stones in close proximity. Tivan assented that they made a wise choice before Sif handed the contained Aether to Tivan, who looked at it in astonishment, before assuring them that the stone would be safe in his possession. [[File:Thor-dark-world-movie-screencaps com-12310.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Tivan ponders about the [[Infinity Stones]]]] As Sif and Volstagg left the Aether in Tivan's care, he observed the Aether and remarked to [[Carina]] that they now had one of the [[Infinity Stones]] in their possession, and only needed the remaining five, hinting that Tivan was already seeking to obtain the Infinity Stones. Tivan started to investigate the whereabouts of the other Infinity stones, in particular, the [[Power Stone]], which was contained inside the [[Orb]] and begun working out a plan to obtain it for himself.{{Ref|TTDW}} ====Buying the Orb==== [[File:Guardians-galaxy-movie-screencaps.com-6652.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Tivan warmly greets [[Gamora]]]] {{Quote|Beautiful, beyond compare."
"We're all very fascinated, whitey. But we'd like to get paid."
"How would you like to get paid?"
"What do you think fancy pants! [[Units]]!|Collector and [[Rocket]]|Guardians of the Galaxy (film)}} In order to obtain the [[Orb]], Tivan hired [[Gamora]] and offered her four billion [[Units]] in exchange for the Orb.{{Ref|GotGPIC}} Together with [[Star-Lord]], [[Drax the Destroyer]], [[Rocket]], and [[Groot]], Gamora brought the Orb to him. Tivan greeted the group warmly, kissing Gamora's hand, but he was particularly fascinated by Groot, as he had never seen ''[[Flora colossi|Flora colossus]]'' before. He offered money for his corpse, although he insisted he would wait until Groot had died first. Rocket Raccoon became defensive of Groot, asking Tivan what he would do with Groot. [[File:Guardians Of The Galaxy NOM0330 comp v073 grade.1110 R.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Collector making his deal with [[Groot]]]] Tivan angered Rocket when he asked if Rocket were the group's pet. As a somewhat intoxicated, irate Rocket attempted to retrieve his gun to silence Tivan, Gamora intervened and reminded Tivan that they had traveled halfway across the galaxy to retrieve the Orb for him, alluding to her arrest on [[Xandar]] and the [[Escape from the Kyln]]. Tivan listened and asked them to show him the Orb so he could add it to his collection. Quill presented the Orb to Tivan, despite having dropped it on the floor. [[File:Collector-explains-the-Stones.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Tivan tells the [[Guardians of the Galaxy|Guardians]] of the [[Infinity Stones]]]] Tivan began opening the [[Orb]], and gave the four outlaws a history of the origin of the [[Infinity Stones]], along with showing them video footage of the [[Power Stone]] being used to destroy all life on a planet. Once the [[Orb]] was fully opened and the Power Stone was revealed to the group, Tivan could not hold back his glee as his hands shook and he remarked how beautiful the stone was. Rocket still was not impressed and demanded that they get their payment. [[File:Collector-survives-explosion.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Tivan survives an explosion]] However, as Tivan was taking out the agreed-upon payment for the Orb to give them, [[Carina]], intending to use the Stone to free herself from his servitude, grasped it to try and become powerful enough to kill Collector. Unable to contain its power, she was killed as the Stone released a massive pulse of energy, which caused her to explode, and destroyed most of Tivan's archive. While Tivan lay in the wreckage, the Guardians took the Orb and escaped from [[Knowhere]].{{Ref|GotG}} ====Recovering from the Explosion==== [[File:CollectorCosmo.png|{{AltFloat}}|Tivan shares a drink with [[Howard the Duck]]]] {{Quote|Why do you let [[Cosmo|it]] lick you like that for? Gross.|[[Howard the Duck]] to Collector|Guardians of the Galaxy (film)}} Tivan survived the destruction, sustaining an injury on the head, which needed to be bandaged. Clearly frustrated with the destruction of his collection, he sat in the debris and shared a drink with one of his former exhibits, [[Howard the Duck]]. [[Cosmo]] also joined them and tried to comfort Tivan by licking his face, an action which was mocked by Howard the Duck.{{Ref|GotG}} ====[[Infinity War]]==== =====[[Ambush in Knowhere|Thanos' Ambush]]===== [[File:The Collector and Thanos.png|{{AltFloat}}|Collector being pinned down by [[Thanos]]]] {{Quote|I told you, I sold [[Reality Stone|it]]. Why would I lie?"
"I imagine it's like breathing for you."
"Like suicide."
"So you do understand.|Collector and [[Thanos]]|Avengers: Infinity War}} In [[2018]], in his [[Infinity War|quest]] for the [[Infinity Stones]], [[Thanos]] traveled to [[Knowhere]] to obtain the [[Reality Stone]] after having procured the [[Power Stone]] and [[Space Stone]] following his [[Massacre of the Xandarians|assault on Xandar]] and [[Attack on the Statesman|ambush on the Statesman]]. Tivan, aware that Thanos would attempt to take the Reality Stone from him, parked [[Collector's Ship|his ship]] in the landing bay of [[Collector's Museum|his museum]] so he could load his collection on to it and evacuate.{{Ref|TAoAIW}} However, before Tivan could escape with his collection, Thanos confronted Tivan and acquired the [[Aether]] from him, extracting the ancient weapon from its container before solidifying it back into its original state. Knowing the [[Guardians of the Galaxy]] would follow and needing [[Gamora]] to recover the [[Soul Stone]], Thanos used the Reality Stone to [[Ambush in Knowhere|bait a trap for them]]. [[File:Collector (Infinity War).png|{{AltFloat}}|Collector notices [[Thanos]]' apparent death]] The Guardians arrived at an intact, yet abandoned, Knowhere where Thanos was attempting to beat information on the Reality Stone out of Tivan's illusion who claimed he had sold the Stone while being ignorant of its true nature. As Thanos admonished Tivan's selfish personality, noting that he was aware that Tivan would broker [[Grandmaster|his brother]] for an extra artifact that he could add to his collection, the Mad [[Titans|Titan]] gave him one last chance to acquiesce to his demands. [[File:Taneleer Tivan (AIW).png|{{AltFloat}}|Collector applauds [[Gamora]]'s "victory" over [[Thanos]]]] When Thanos heard [[Drax the Destroyer]] falling to the ground after being put to sleep by [[Mantis]], he threw Collector into a specimen cage. Gamora then attacked Thanos and seemingly killed him, causing Tivan to applaud her victory before the real Thanos revealed himself and dropped the illusion. Tivan disappeared at the same time, revealing that he was an illusion and Knowhere was truly up in flames.{{Ref|AIW}} {{Clear}} ===Selling Knowhere=== {{Quote|Ever since buying [[Knowhere]] from the Collector, we don't have time for trivialities like Christmas.|[[Nebula]]|The Guardians of the Galaxy Holiday Special}} Tivan sold what remained of [[Knowhere]] to the [[Guardians of the Galaxy]].{{Ref|TGotGHS}} ==Powers and Abilities== ===Powers=== *'''Superhuman Durability''': Tivan's body is much more resistant to physical harm than the body of a human being. When [[Carina]] used the [[Orb]], it released a massive pulse of energy, which caused her to explode, and destroyed most of Tivan's archive. He survived the destruction with only some bruising and a bad hit on the head which just needed to be bandaged. Despite having durability greater than normal beings, he was eventually defeated in the hands of Thanos. *'''Nigh-Immortality''': Collector is nearly as old as the Universe itself, albeit he can still die. ===Abilities=== *'''Genius-Level Intellect''': [[File:CollectorInfinityStones.png|thumb|250px|Collector explaining the [[Infinity Stones]]]] Collector has vast knowledge on the universe, being able to narrate the history of the [[Infinity Stones]] to the [[Guardians of the Galaxy]]. *'''Master Businessperson''': Tivan is a very intelligent businessman. He formed a deal with [[Gamora]] to give her millions of units in exchange for the [[Power Stone]] to widen his collection. He also made a deal with the [[Asgardians]] to keep the [[Reality Stone]] safe. *'''Tactician''': Collector formulated a plan to gather all six [[Infinity Stones]] for unknown purposes, however, he could only get the [[Reality Stone]] before it was taken by [[Thanos]]. ==Equipment== ===Other Equipment=== *'''[[Birth Pods]]''': Collector had a primitive Birth Pod from a [[Sovereign]], which he kept in his museum. *'''[[Reality Stone]]''': Given by the [[Warriors Three]], Collector was entrusted with the Aether, a liquified-form of the Reality Stone, shortly after the [[Second Dark Elf Conflict]] because the [[Asgardians]] deemed it unwise to keep two [[Infinity Stones]] in proximity as the [[Tesseract]] was in [[Odin's Vault]]. However, Thanos stole the Aether from the [[Collector's Museum]] four years later and added it to his [[Infinity Gauntlet|gauntlet]]. *'''[[Power Stone]]''': Despite having hired Gamora to retrieve the [[Orb]] for him, Collector never owned the Power Stone as [[Carina]] attempted to use its power to free herself despite being killed. ===Vehicles=== *'''[[Collector's Ship]]''': Collector had his own personal ship that he parked at his [[Collector's Museum|museum]] on [[Knowhere]]. The ship was destroyed during [[Thanos]]'s [[Ambush in Knowhere|assault on the museum]] in search of the [[Reality Stone]]. ==Facilities== {{Quote|I can assure you, [[Reality Stone|it]] will be absolutely safe here in my collection.|Collector to [[Sif]]|Thor: The Dark World}} *'''[[Collector's Museum]]''': [[File:Guardians-galaxy-movie-screencaps.com-6634.jpg|thumb|250px|Collector admiring his entire collection]] Collector's Museum was where Tivan would store the various objects and specimens in his collection. Located in [[Knowhere]], the [[Asgardians]] went to the museum to deliver the [[Aether]] to Tivan. The [[Guardians of the Galaxy]] brought the [[Orb]] to the museum to sell it and Tivan explained to them that it contained an [[Power Stone|Infinity Stone]]. Upon learning this, [[Carina]] committed suicide by grabbing the stone, causing an explosion, resulting in the escape of all his creatures. Years later, [[Thanos]] traveled to the museum, and stole the Aether from Tivan. ==Relationships== {{col-begin}}{{col-2}} ===Family=== *[[Grandmaster|En Dwi Gast/Grandmaster]] - Brother *[[Carlo (Cousin)|Carlo]] † - Cousin ===Allies=== *[[Tivan Group]] **[[Steemie Blueliver]] *[[Asgardians]] **[[Sif]] **[[Volstagg]] † *[[Broker]] *[[Guardians of the Galaxy]] **[[Star-Lord|Peter Quill/Star-Lord]] **[[Gamora]] † **[[Rocket]] **[[Groot]] **[[Mantis]] **[[Nebula]] **[[Kraglin Obfonteri]] **[[Cosmo]] - Former Collection Piece *[[Howard the Duck]] - Former Collection Piece {{col-2}} ===Enemies=== *[[Krylorian Attendant]] † - Former Slave and Collection Piece *[[Carina]] † - Former Slave and Attempted Killer *[[Thanos]] † {{col-end}} ==Appearances== {| class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wikitable" width="100%" ! Appearances for Collector |- | {{Scroll box|content= In chronological order: *{{Appearance|TTDW}} **{{Appearance|GotGPIC}} (flashbacks; concurrent events) **{{Appearance|AIWP2}} (flashbacks) *{{Appearance|GotGPIC}} *{{Appearance|GotG}} **{{Appearance|GotGV2P1}} **{{Appearance|GotGRASaCD}} *{{Appearance|GotGV2RR}} *{{Appearance|AIW}} (mentioned) **{{Appearance|AEP1}} (mentioned) **{{Appearance|AEP2}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|TGotGHS}} (mentioned) }} |} ==Trivia== *In the comics, Collector is one of the [[:w:c:marvel:Elders of the Universe|Elders of the Universe]], a group which is comprised of the last survivors of extinct races from across the universe, making them the oldest individuals in the comics. He dedicated his life to collect artifacts and living beings to place them out of danger as a goal for his life, as his immortality is directly linked to his will to live. ==Behind the Scenes== *[[Benicio del Toro]] was uncredited for his appearance in ''[[Thor: The Dark World]]''. *Describing [[Benicio del Toro]]'s performance, [[James Gunn]] said "''He's like an outer-space {{WPS|Liberace}}. That's what it says in the script, which he's kind of doing''".[http://www.mtv.com/news/1716870/guardians-of-the-galaxy-director-james-gunn/ 'GUARDIANS OF THE GALAXY': LATEST ON VIN, BRADLEY AND THE SCENE EVERYONE'S TALKING ABOUT] *[[Oliver Gough]] was a stunt double for [[Benicio del Toro]] in the role of Collector. *[[Benicio del Toro]] was early reported by [[Zoe Saldana]] to be reprising his role as Collector in ''[[Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2]]''.[http://collider.com/guardians-of-the-galaxy-2-benicio-del-toro-returning/ ‘Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2’: Zoe Saldana Confirms Benicio Del Toro Will Return] However, del Toro later confirmed that he would not be appearing in the second film.[https://www.comingsoon.net/movies/news/646537-collector-guardians-of-the-galaxy-vol-2#/slide/1 Don’t Expect the Collector in Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2] ==References== {{Gallery|Collector/Gallery}} {{Wq|Collector}} {{Reflist}} ==External Links== *{{Marvel}} *{{WP|Collector (comics)|Collector}} *{{Marvel.com|characters/collector-taneleer-tavan}} [[ru:Коллекционер]] [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:Thor: The Dark World Characters]] [[Category:Guardians of the Galaxy (film) Characters]] [[Category:Avengers: Infinity War Characters]] [[Category:Comic Characters]] [[Category:Book Characters]] [[Category:Unknown Race]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:White Hair]] [[Category:Brown Eyes]] [[Category:Stonekeepers]] [[Category:Tivan Group Leaders]]
